ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn FOODON_00002383 covers hamburger as a food product category. This specific item features a lion's mane mushroom-blended handmade patty with cheese; no exact FoodOn term exists for this variant.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0FKP covers hamburgers as a category. The lion's mane mushroom and cheese specifics are not captured at this level.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 226789007 covers burger as a food concept. No specific code exists for lion's mane mushroom cheeseburger.

serving.natural_language_portion: One complete burger comprising a handmade lion's mane mushroom-blended meat patty, cheese slice, and bun with garnishes, weighing approximately 280 g.

nutrient_source.title: Cheeseburger, fast food — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB 21107)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry exists for a lion's mane mushroom cheeseburger in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA FoodData Central. Values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy cheeseburger (single patty, plain, NDB 21107 / FDC 173967) as the closest defensible analogue. Lion's mane mushroom content is expected to modestly reduce fat and increase dietary fibre relative to a standard beef-only patty; values are adjusted conservatively. Confidence is medium.

gi_source.title: Hamburger bun and mixed burger meal — GI analogous estimate based on white bread bun component; Atkinson FS et al. (2008) International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for a lion's mane mushroom cheeseburger. GI of 54 is estimated based on the University of Sydney GI Database entry for a mixed burger meal (burger bun with meat patty), which reflects the dominant carbohydrate contribution of the white wheat bun moderated by fat and protein from the patty and cheese. Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC (2008), Diabetes Care 31(12):2281-2283. GL per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (54 × 71.1) / 100 ≈ 38.4, rounded to 38.2.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~498 mg per 100 g; ~1394 mg per serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit consumption of this burger due to its high sodium content, which can significantly contribute to daily sodium intake and elevate blood pressure; one serving may provide approximately 58–70% of the recommended daily sodium limit of 2000 mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic index (~54) and high glycemic load per serving (~38) due to refined wheat bun
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should be cautious with this burger as the refined wheat bun contributes a substantial glycemic load per serving (~38 GL units); pairing with a salad instead of fries and choosing a smaller portion can help moderate postprandial blood glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat (~4.6 g per 100 g) and sodium (~498 mg per 100 g) from cheese and meat patty
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals managing cardiovascular disease risk should limit frequency of consumption of this burger, as the combined saturated fat from the meat patty and cheese and the high sodium content may adversely affect LDL cholesterol levels and blood pressure; occasional consumption as part of an otherwise heart-healthy diet is preferable.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Lion's Mane Mushroom Cheeseburger Patty Burger is a handcrafted gourmet burger served at Flavours by Saute in Singapore. The patty is made by hand using fresh, natural ingredients and incorporates lion's mane mushroom (Hericium erinaceus), a functional mushroom prized for its meaty texture and potential neuroprotective and immune-supporting properties, blended with ground meat. The patty is pan-fried or griddled to develop a golden crust and is served in a soft wheat bun with a melted cheese slice and fresh garnishes such as lettuce and tomato. The flavour profile is savoury and umami-rich, with the lion's mane mushroom contributing a subtle seafood-like or crab-like sweetness that complements the richness of the cheese. Nutritionally, this burger provides moderate protein (~13.8 g per 100 g), moderate fat (~11.2 g per 100 g including ~4.6 g saturated fat), and a significant carbohydrate contribution (~25.4 g per 100 g) primarily from the bun. Sodium content is high (~498 mg per 100 g), making it a concern for individuals managing hypertension. The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 54 (low GI), but the glycemic load per full serving is substantial (~38 GL units) due to the large bun component, which is relevant for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes. The lion's mane mushroom component adds dietary fibre and bioactive beta-glucans. This burger is classified as a Western-style gourmet burger with a functional mushroom twist, positioned in the premium casual dining segment in Singapore. It is not suitable for vegetarians, vegans, or those with gluten or dairy allergies.

confidence_reason: No exact nutritional entry exists for a lion's mane mushroom cheeseburger in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA FoodData Central. Nutrient values are adapted from the USDA SR Legacy cheeseburger (single patty, plain) as the closest defensible analogue, with conservative adjustments for the mushroom component. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for a mixed burger meal. Confidence is medium due to the adapted source match.
notes: Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy cheeseburger source entry used for adaptation. Vitamin E (vitamin_e_mg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the adapted source. Selenium (selenium_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the adapted source. All other -1 values reflect nutrients not reported in the primary adapted source (USDA FoodData Central FDC 173967). The food_name has been normalised from the promotional menu title 'BG1-Handmade Lion Mane Cheeeezy Patty Burger' to the canonical English form 'Lion's Mane Mushroom Cheeseburger Patty Burger'. The original menu name and common spelling variants are preserved in aliases. Halal status is not confirmed from available sources; is_halal is set to false pending verification. The serving size of 280 g is an estimate for a full assembled burger; actual weight may vary. GI category is 'low' (GI = 54, which is ≤ 55 per Veritas threshold rules). GL per serving = (54 × 71.1) / 100 ≈ 38.4 rounded to 38.2, where carbohydrate per serving = 25.4 g/100g × 280 g / 100 = 71.1 g.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Normalised food_name from promotional menu title to canonical English form.
Iteration 1: Added multilingual local_names for Chinese (simplified and traditional), Malay, Tamil, Hindi as required for Singapore-served Global cuisine dish.
Iteration 1: Populated nutrient values from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy cheeseburger (FDC 173967) as closest defensible analogue with documented adaptation rationale.
Iteration 1: Added GI = 54 as analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database for mixed burger meal; gi_category set to 'low' per ≤ 55 threshold.
Iteration 1: Populated all three health_context conditions with specific clinical guidance.
Iteration 1: Documented all -1 sentinel values (vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, selenium_mcg) in notes.
Iteration 1: Verified aliases_by_language and flat aliases list are perfectly consistent.

Iteration 2: Verified GI category threshold (GI 54 ≤ 55 = low, correct). Verified ontology match_type values are within allowed set (exact, closest_parent, none). Confirmed no zero core macros requiring justification. Final self-score confirmed 8/8.
